projects.title: Add Two Numbers
projects.description: Write a function that takes two integers and returns their sum.
projects.logicExplanation: Simple addition: return the sum of two integers.
projects.codeExplanation: The function `add()` takes two int parameters and returns their sum. In `main()`, the user inputs two numbers, calls the function, and prints the result.

projects.title: Subtract Two Numbers
projects.description: Write a function that subtracts the second integer from the first and returns the result.
projects.logicExplanation: Subtraction: return the difference of two integers.
projects.codeExplanation: The function `subtract()` takes two ints and returns the result of `a - b`. `main()` handles I/O.

projects.title: Multiply Two Numbers
projects.description: Write a function that multiplies two integers and returns the product.
projects.logicExplanation: Multiplication: return the product of two integers.
projects.codeExplanation: `multiply()` returns `a * b`. `main()` reads two numbers and prints the product.

projects.title: Divide Two Numbers (Integer Division)
projects.description: Write a function that divides the first integer by the second (non‑zero) and returns the integer quotient.
projects.logicExplanation: Integer division: return the quotient of two integers (assuming divisor non‑zero).
projects.codeExplanation: `divide()` returns `a / b`. `main()` checks for division by zero (optional) and prints the quotient.

projects.title: Factorial (Iterative)
projects.description: Write a function to compute the factorial of a non‑negative integer using iteration.
projects.logicExplanation: Factorial of n computed iteratively by multiplying from 1 to n.
projects.codeExplanation: `factorialIter()` uses a loop to accumulate product. `main()` reads n and prints result.

projects.title: Factorial (Recursive)
projects.description: Write a recursive function to compute the factorial of a non‑negative integer.
projects.logicExplanation: Factorial of n (n!) defined recursively: n! = n * (n-1)! with 0! = 1.
projects.codeExplanation: `factorialRec()` calls itself with n-1 until base case. `main()` reads n and prints result.

projects.title: Check Prime Number
projects.description: Write a function that returns 1 if a number is prime, 0 otherwise.
projects.logicExplanation: A prime number is greater than 1 and divisible only by 1 and itself. Check divisibility up to sqrt(n).
projects.codeExplanation: `isPrime()` handles numbers <2, then loops from 2 to sqrt(n). Returns 1 if prime, 0 otherwise. `main()` reads a number and prints the result.

projects.title: Check Even or Odd
projects.description: Write a function that returns 1 if a number is even, 0 if odd.
projects.logicExplanation: An even number is divisible by 2; an odd number is not.
projects.codeExplanation: `isEven()` returns 1 if number % 2 == 0 else 0. `main()` reads a number and prints 'Even' or 'Odd'.

projects.title: Maximum of Two Numbers
projects.description: Write a function that returns the larger of two integers.
projects.logicExplanation: Compare two numbers and return the larger one.
projects.codeExplanation: `max()` returns a if a > b else b. `main()` reads two numbers and prints the maximum.

projects.title: Minimum of Two Numbers
projects.description: Write a function that returns the smaller of two integers.
projects.logicExplanation: Compare two numbers and return the smaller one.
projects.codeExplanation: `min()` returns a if a < b else b. `main()` reads two numbers and prints the minimum.

projects.title: Power (Iterative)
projects.description: Write a function to compute base raised to exponent (non‑negative) using iteration.
projects.logicExplanation: Compute base raised to exponent by repeated multiplication.
projects.codeExplanation: `powerIter()` uses a loop to multiply base exponent times. `main()` reads base and exponent and prints result.

projects.title: Power (Recursive)
projects.description: Write a recursive function to compute base raised to exponent (non‑negative).
projects.logicExplanation: Recursive power: base^exp = base * base^(exp-1) with base^0 = 1.
projects.codeExplanation: `powerRec()` calls itself with decreasing exponent. `main()` reads base and exponent and prints result.

projects.title: Sum of Digits
projects.description: Write a function that returns the sum of the digits of a positive integer.
projects.learningOutcome: Digit extraction using modulo and division.
projects.logicExplanation: Extract digits by repeatedly taking modulo 10 and dividing by 10, accumulating the sum.
projects.codeExplanation: `sumOfDigits()` uses a while loop to add last digit and remove it. `main()` reads a number and prints the sum.

projects.title: Reverse a Number
projects.description: Write a function that returns the reverse of a given integer.
projects.logicExplanation: Build the reversed number by repeatedly taking the last digit and appending it.
projects.codeExplanation: `reverseNumber()` uses a loop: rev = rev*10 + digit. `main()` reads a number and prints the reversed number.

projects.title: Palindrome Number
projects.description: Write a function that checks if a number is a palindrome (reads the same forwards and backwards).
projects.logicExplanation: A palindrome reads the same forwards and backwards. Compare the number with its reverse.
projects.codeExplanation: `isPalindrome()` reverses the number and compares with original. `main()` reads a number and prints whether it's a palindrome.

projects.title: Fibonacci (Iterative)
projects.description: Write a function to return the nth Fibonacci number using iteration.
projects.logicExplanation: Fibonacci computed iteratively using a loop and two variables.
projects.codeExplanation: `fibIter()` uses a for loop to build the sequence up to n. `main()` reads n and prints the nth Fibonacci number.

projects.title: Fibonacci (Recursive)
projects.description: Write a recursive function to return the nth Fibonacci number.
projects.codeExplanation: `fibRec()` calls itself recursively. `main()` reads n and prints the nth Fibonacci number.

projects.title: GCD (Euclidean Algorithm)
projects.description: Write a function to compute the greatest common divisor of two integers using the Euclidean algorithm.
projects.logicExplanation: Greatest Common Divisor using Euclidean algorithm: GCD(a,b) = GCD(b, a%b).
projects.codeExplanation: `gcd()` uses recursion or loop to apply the Euclidean algorithm. `main()` reads two numbers and prints their GCD.

projects.title: LCM
projects.description: Write a function to compute the least common multiple of two integers.
projects.logicExplanation: Least Common Multiple: LCM(a,b) = (a*b) / GCD(a,b).
projects.codeExplanation: `lcm()` first computes GCD using a helper function, then applies the formula. `main()` reads two numbers and prints LCM.

projects.title: Count Digits
projects.description: Write a function that returns the number of digits in a positive integer.
projects.logicExplanation: Count the number of digits in a positive integer by repeatedly dividing by 10.
projects.codeExplanation: `countDigits()` uses a while loop to divide until zero, counting steps. `main()` reads a number and prints the digit count.

projects.title: Armstrong Number
projects.description: Write a function to check if a number is an Armstrong number (sum of its digits each raised to the power of number of digits equals the number).
projects.logicExplanation: An Armstrong number (narcissistic) equals the sum of its digits each raised to the power of the number of digits.
projects.codeExplanation: `isArmstrong()` counts digits, then computes sum of each digit raised to that power, and compares with original. `main()` reads a number and prints result.

projects.title: Perfect Number
projects.description: Write a function to check if a number is perfect (equal to sum of its proper divisors).
projects.logicExplanation: A perfect number equals the sum of its proper divisors (excluding itself).
projects.codeExplanation: `isPerfect()` loops from 1 to n/2, sums divisors, and checks if sum equals n. `main()` reads a number and prints result.

projects.title: Sum of Array Elements
projects.description: Write a function that takes an integer array and its size, and returns the sum of all elements.
projects.learningOutcome: Array traversal, function with array parameter.
projects.logicExplanation: Iterate through the array and accumulate the total.
projects.codeExplanation: `sumArray()` takes an array and its size, uses a for loop to add each element, and returns the sum. `main()` reads array and prints sum.

projects.title: Average of Array Elements
projects.description: Write a function that takes an integer array and its size, and returns the average as a float.
projects.logicExplanation: Compute the average as sum of elements divided by number of elements.
projects.codeExplanation: `averageArray()` calls sumArray() (or computes sum directly) and divides by n (using float division). `main()` reads array and prints average.

projects.title: Linear Search
projects.description: Write a function that searches for a key in an array. Return the index if found, otherwise -1.
projects.exampleText:
Enter size: 6
Enter elements: 2 4 6 8 10 12
Enter key: 8
projects.logicExplanation: Search for a key in an array by checking each element sequentially. Return index if found, else -1.
projects.codeExplanation: `linearSearch()` loops through array, compares each element with key, returns index if found. `main()` reads array and key, prints result.

projects.title: Demonstrate Constants (const and #define)
projects.description: Write a program to calculate the area of a circle using PI defined as a constant via const and #define. Show both methods.
projects.learningOutcome: Understanding constants in C, const keyword, preprocessor #define.
projects.logicExplanation: Use both `const` and `#define` to define the constant PI, then compute area of a circle.
projects.codeExplanation: Two methods: `const double PI = 3.14159;` and `#define PI 3.14159`. The program reads radius, calculates area using both, and prints results.

projects.title: Type Conversion (Implicit and Explicit)
projects.description: Demonstrate implicit conversion and explicit casting in C.
projects.exampleText:
Enter an integer: 7
Enter a float: 3.14
projects.exampleOutput:
Implicit: int / int stored in float: 7/2 = 3.000000
Explicit cast: (float)7/2 = 3.500000
Casting float to int: (int)3.14 = 3
projects.learningOutcome: Implicit type conversion, explicit casting, type promotion.
projects.logicExplanation: Demonstrate implicit conversion (e.g., int to float in division) and explicit casting (e.g., (float)int).
projects.codeExplanation: Program shows: implicit conversion when dividing int by int stored in float, and explicit cast to get float division. Also shows truncation when casting float to int.

projects.title: Relational Operators – Compare Two Numbers
projects.description: Use all relational operators to compare two integers and print the results (1 for true, 0 for false).
projects.learningOutcome: Understanding relational operators and their output.
projects.logicExplanation: Use all relational operators (>, <, >=, <=, ==, !=) to compare two integers and print the results.
projects.codeExplanation: Read two numbers, then print the result of each relational operation (1 for true, 0 for false).

projects.title: Logical Operators – Leap Year Check
projects.description: Check if a given year is a leap year using logical operators (&&, ||).
projects.learningOutcome: Combining conditions with logical AND and OR.
projects.logicExplanation: A year is a leap year if divisible by 400 or (divisible by 4 and not by 100). Use logical && and ||.
projects.codeExplanation: `isLeap()` returns 1 if year satisfies leap condition. In main, read year and print result.

projects.title: Compound Assignment Operators
projects.description: Demonstrate the use of +=, -=, *=, /=, %= on a variable.
projects.logicExplanation: Demonstrate +=, -=, *=, /=, %= on a variable.
projects.codeExplanation: Start with a variable, apply each compound operator with a value, and print the result after each step.

projects.title: Increment and Decrement Operators (Prefix vs Postfix)
projects.description: Show the difference between prefix and postfix increment/decrement.
projects.learningOutcome: Prefix and postfix operators and their side effects.
projects.logicExplanation: Show difference between prefix (++i) and postfix (i++) increment/decrement.
projects.codeExplanation: Declare a variable, print its value using prefix and postfix in expressions, and explain the output.

projects.title: Formatted Input with scanf – Read Multiple Types
projects.description: Use scanf to read an integer, a float, and a character, then print them back.
projects.exampleText:
Enter an integer: 10
Enter a float: 3.14
Enter a character: A
projects.exampleOutput: You entered: integer = 10, float = 3.140000, character = A
projects.learningOutcome: Using format specifiers with scanf for different data types.
projects.logicExplanation: Use scanf to read different data types (int, float, char) with appropriate format specifiers.
projects.codeExplanation: Read an integer, a float, and a character, then print them back using printf with proper format.

projects.title: Expression Precedence – Evaluate Complex Expression
projects.description: Evaluate an expression involving multiple operators to understand precedence and associativity.
projects.exampleText: Enter a, b, c, d, e: 2 3 4 5 6
projects.logicExplanation: Evaluate a complex expression involving multiple operators to understand precedence and associativity.
projects.codeExplanation: Compute an expression like a + b * c - d / e and print the result, explaining the order.

projects.title: While Loop – Print Numbers 1 to N
projects.description: Print numbers from 1 to N using a while loop.
projects.logicExplanation: Use a while loop to print numbers from 1 to N.
projects.codeExplanation: Initialize counter, while condition, print and increment inside loop.

projects.title: While Loop – Sum of First N Natural Numbers
projects.description: Compute the sum of first N natural numbers using a while loop.
projects.logicExplanation: Compute sum of first N natural numbers using a while loop.
projects.codeExplanation: Accumulate sum in a variable while iterating from 1 to N.

projects.title: Do-While Loop – Menu-Driven Calculator
projects.description: Create a menu-driven calculator that repeats until user chooses exit, using a do-while loop.
projects.exampleText:
1. Add
2. Subtract
3. Multiply
4. Divide
5. Exit
Enter choice: 1
Enter two numbers: 10 5
Result = 15
projects.learningOutcome: Do-while loop for menus, switch statement.
projects.logicExplanation: Present a menu of operations (add, subtract, etc.) and repeat until user chooses exit, using do-while.
projects.codeExplanation: Display menu, read choice, perform operation using switch, loop while choice != exit.

projects.title: Do-While Loop – Guess the Number Game
projects.description: Generate a random number between 1 and 100, and let the user guess it with hints, using do-while.
projects.exampleText:
Guess the number (1-100): 50
Too low!
Guess again: 75
Too high!
...
projects.exampleOutput: Congratulations! You guessed it in X tries.
projects.learningOutcome: Random numbers, do-while for at least one iteration.
projects.logicExplanation: Generate a random number, let user guess, give hints, repeat until correct using do-while.
projects.codeExplanation: Use rand() to generate number, do-while loop to keep asking, compare guess and give feedback.

projects.title: For Loop – Multiplication Table
projects.description: Print the multiplication table of a given number using a for loop.
projects.logicExplanation: Print multiplication table of a given number using for loop.
projects.codeExplanation: Loop from 1 to 10, print number * i.

projects.title: For Loop – Sum of Squares Series
projects.description: Compute the sum of squares: 1^2 + 2^2 + ... + n^2 using a for loop.
projects.logicExplanation: Compute sum of squares 1^2 + 2^2 + ... + n^2 using for loop.
projects.codeExplanation: Accumulate i*i in a loop.

projects.title: Break in Loop – Find First Number Divisible by 7 and 5
projects.description: Find the first number in a given range that is divisible by both 7 and 5 using break.
projects.exampleOutput: First number divisible by 7 and 5 is 35
projects.learningOutcome: Using break to exit loop early.
projects.logicExplanation: Find the first number in a range that is divisible by both 7 and 5 using break.
projects.codeExplanation: Loop from a start to end, check condition, if found print and break.

projects.title: Continue in Loop – Skip Multiples of 3
projects.description: Print numbers from 1 to 20, skipping multiples of 3 using continue.
projects.exampleText: Numbers from 1 to 20 skipping multiples of 3:
projects.logicExplanation: Print numbers from 1 to 20 skipping multiples of 3 using continue.
projects.codeExplanation: Loop 1 to 20, if i%3==0 continue, else print.

projects.title: Nested Loops – Right-Angled Triangle Pattern
projects.description: Print a right-angled triangle of stars using nested loops.
projects.logicExplanation: Print a right-angled triangle of stars with nested loops.
projects.codeExplanation: Outer loop for rows, inner loop for columns, print stars accordingly.

projects.title: Nested Loops – Pyramid Pattern
projects.description: Print a centered pyramid of stars using nested loops.
projects.learningOutcome: Nested loops with spaces and stars.
projects.logicExplanation: Print a centered pyramid pattern using nested loops.
projects.codeExplanation: Outer loop for rows, inner loops for spaces and stars.

projects.title: Nested Loops – Diamond Pattern
projects.description: Print a diamond pattern of stars using nested loops.
projects.logicExplanation: Print a diamond pattern using nested loops.
projects.codeExplanation: Two parts: upper pyramid and lower inverted pyramid.

projects.title: Nested Loops – Multiplication Table Grid
projects.description: Print a 10x10 multiplication table grid using nested loops.
projects.logicExplanation: Print a 10x10 multiplication table grid using nested loops.
projects.codeExplanation: Outer loop for rows 1-10, inner for columns 1-10, print product formatted.

projects.title: While vs Do-While – Demonstrate Difference
projects.description: Show that a do-while loop executes at least once even if the condition is false initially, while a while loop may not execute.
projects.exampleOutput:
While loop with condition false: (no output)
Do-while with same condition: This executes once!
projects.logicExplanation: Demonstrate that do-while executes at least once even if condition is false initially.
projects.codeExplanation: Two loops: while with false condition (no output) and do-while with same condition (executes once).

projects.title: For Loop with Multiple Counters – Nested Pairs
projects.description: Use nested for loops to print pairs (i, j) for i = 1..3 and j = 1..3.
projects.logicExplanation: Use for loop with multiple counters to print pairs (i,j) for i=1..3, j=1..3.
projects.codeExplanation: Nested for loops, print i and j.

projects.title: Simple Interest Calculation
projects.description: Calculate simple interest given principal, rate, and time.
projects.logicExplanation: Calculate simple interest: SI = (P * R * T) / 100.
projects.codeExplanation: Read principal, rate, time; compute and print interest.

projects.title: Temperature Conversion (C to F and F to C)
projects.description: Convert Celsius to Fahrenheit and Fahrenheit to Celsius using a menu.
projects.exampleText:
1. C to F
2. F to C
Enter choice: 1
Enter temperature in C: 100
projects.logicExplanation: Convert Celsius to Fahrenheit and vice versa using formulas.
projects.codeExplanation: Menu-driven: choose conversion, read temperature, apply formula, print result.

projects.title: Print ASCII Values of Uppercase Letters
projects.description: Print the ASCII values of uppercase letters A to Z using a loop.
projects.logicExplanation: Print ASCII values of uppercase letters A to Z.
projects.codeExplanation: Loop from 'A' to 'Z', print character and its integer value.
